hero.title: The Rip
hero.overview: Trust frays when a team of Miami cops discovers millions in cash inside a run-down stash house, calling everyone — and everything — into question.

forecast_cards.title: The Rip
forecast_cards.overview: Trust frays when a team of Miami cops discovers millions in cash inside a run-down stash house, calling everyone — and everything — into question.

forecast_cards.title: Project Hail Mary
forecast_cards.overview: Science teacher Ryland Grace wakes up on a spaceship light years from home with no recollection of who he is or how he got there. As his memory returns, he begins to uncover his mission: solve the riddle of the mysterious substance causing the sun to die out. He must call on his scientific knowledge and unorthodox ideas to save everything on Earth from extinction… but an unexpected friendship means he may not have to do it alone.

forecast_cards.title: People We Meet on Vacation
forecast_cards.overview: Poppy's a free spirit. Alex loves a plan. After years of summer vacations, these polar-opposite pals wonder if they could be a perfect romantic match.

forecast_cards.title: The Bride!
forecast_cards.overview: A lonely Frankenstein travels to 1930s Chicago to ask groundbreaking scientist Dr. Euphronious to create a companion for him. The two revive a murdered young woman and The Bride is born. But what ensues is beyond what either of them imagined.

forecast_cards.title: The Wrecking Crew
forecast_cards.overview: Estranged half-brothers Jonny and James reunite after their father's mysterious death. As they search for the truth, buried secrets reveal a conspiracy threatening to tear their family apart.

forecast_cards.title: War Machine
forecast_cards.overview: On one last grueling mission during Army Ranger training, a combat engineer must lead his unit in a fight against a giant otherworldly killing machine.

forecast_cards.title: The Drama
forecast_cards.overview: A happily engaged couple is put to the test when an unexpected turn sends their wedding week off the rails.

forecast_cards.title: Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man
forecast_cards.overview: After his estranged son gets embroiled in a Nazi plot, self-exiled gangster Tommy Shelby must return to Birmingham to save his family — and his nation.

forecast_cards.title: Crime 101
forecast_cards.overview: When an elusive thief whose high-stakes heists unfold along the iconic 101 freeway in Los Angeles eyes the score of a lifetime, with hopes of this being his final job, his path collides with a disillusioned insurance broker who is facing her own crossroads. Determined to crack the case, a relentless detective closes in on the operation, raising the stakes even higher.

forecast_cards.title: Send Help
forecast_cards.overview: Two colleagues become stranded on a deserted island, the only survivors of a plane crash. On the island, they must overcome past grievances and work together to survive, but ultimately, it's a battle of wills and wits to make it out alive.

forecast_cards.title: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple
forecast_cards.overview: Dr. Kelson finds himself in a shocking new relationship - with consequences that could change the world as they know it - and Spike's encounter with Jimmy Crystal becomes a nightmare he can't escape.

forecast_cards.title: "Wuthering Heights"
forecast_cards.overview: Tragedy strikes when Heathcliff falls in love with Catherine Earnshaw, a woman from a wealthy family in 18th-century England.

rows.film: All Quiet on the Western Front

historical_years.forecast_mode_summary: The earliest board is meant to identify plausible contenders, not lock the winner. It should move a lot as real reviews, festivals, and awards data arrive.
historical_years.forecast_mode_leans_on: release timing, prestige profile, curated contender flags, early critic signal, and broad festival positioning
historical_years.forecast_mode_best_for: starting the field early and spotting which films belong on the board at all

recent_races.runner_up: All Quiet on the Western Front

top_three.film: All Quiet on the Western Front

season_modes.label: Early
season_modes.summary: The earliest board is meant to identify plausible contenders, not lock the winner. It should move a lot as real reviews, festivals, and awards data arrive.
season_modes.leans_on: release timing, prestige profile, curated contender flags, early critic signal, and broad festival positioning
season_modes.best_for: starting the field early and spotting which films belong on the board at all
season_modes.status_summary: The 2027 Oscars board is currently running in this mode.

season_modes.label: Festival
season_modes.summary: Once Venice, Telluride, TIFF, and fall launches start landing, the board should react more to real reception and premiere strength than to pure preseason priors.
season_modes.leans_on: festival launches, critic scores, distributor strength, and early crowd response
season_modes.best_for: sorting serious contenders from the larger preseason pool
season_modes.status_summary: This mode should only turn on once the race reaches festival season.

season_modes.label: Precursor
season_modes.summary: During guild and critics season, the forecast should behave more like an awards race and less like a release calendar watchlist.
season_modes.leans_on: PGA, DGA, Critics Choice, BAFTA, SAG, Golden Globe signals, plus critics and nomination volume
season_modes.best_for: tracking the real shape of the race before nominations lock
season_modes.status_summary: This mode should only turn on once the race reaches precursor season.

season_modes.label: Post Nomination
season_modes.summary: After Oscar nominations, the board should be closest to a true winner model because the final field is known and precursor strength matters most.
season_modes.leans_on: Oscar nominations, precursor wins, critic scores, and late-race package strength
season_modes.best_for: final winner forecasting after the field is set
season_modes.status_summary: This mode should only turn on once the race reaches post nomination season.

actual_winners.film: Birdman or (The Unexpected Virtue of Ignorance)
The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King

backtest_rows.runner_up: All Quiet on the Western Front

methodology.headline: The site uses a production Best Picture model trained on modern-era nominees, plus a longer walk-forward validation window to keep the headline honest.
bullets: Walk-forward means each historical film year is scored only with models trained on earlier film years. The live production setup starts training at 2010 and currently scores 2020-2025.
The longer validation view starts training at 2005 so we can review a tougher 11-year window from 2015-2025.

The winner model is trained on Oscar nomination totals, precursor wins and nominations from PGA, DGA, Critics Choice, BAFTA, SAG, and the Golden Globes, plus critics scores, release timing, distributor flags, genre tags, festivals, and director history.

The simple comparison baseline picks the film with the most precursor wins, then precursor nominations, then Oscar nominations, Metacritic, and Rotten Tomatoes. That baseline is shown alongside the model on the longer validation window.
Confidence labels are now shrinkage-calibrated toward prior walk-forward accuracy. On the 2015-2024 extended window, top-pick Brier improved from 0.195 to 0.198.
Future races rely on TMDb-first movie pool data and a season-aware blend that shifts weight from broad prestige tracking early in the year toward awards-style signals later in the race.
